subject: Redis cluster master node crashed
description: The master node of Redis cluster redis-cluster-01 crashed suddenly at 2026-03-25 14:23. Sentinel failed to complete failover within expected time (timeout configured at 30s, actual wait exceeded 2 minutes). Cause: Master node ran out of memory (used_memory: 16GB, maxmemory: 16GB), triggering OOM Killer. Impact: All services depending on Redis experienced widespread failures.

comments.author: Infrastructure Team
comments.text: Manual failover completed; replica redis-slave-02 promoted to master. Outage lasted approximately 8 minutes.

subject: user-service widespread unavailability
description: user-service returned 503 errors in bulk after 14:23, error rate surged from 0.1% to 78%. User login, session validation, and user info queries all affected. Error logs show: redis.clients.jedis.exceptions.JedisConnectionException: Could not get a resource from the pool. user-service depends on Redis for session storage and user cache.

comments.author: User Team
comments.text: user-service has no local cache fallback mechanism; when Redis is unavailable, it cannot serve any requests.

subject: order-service order endpoint timeouts
description: The POST /orders endpoint of order-service started timing out in bulk from 14:24 (P99 rose from 200ms to 30s). Error rate reached 65%. Log analysis: order-service needs to call user-service to verify user info before creating orders; user-service being unavailable caused order-service thread pool exhaustion. order-service HTTP client timeout is configured at 30s with no circuit breaker.

comments.author: Order Team
comments.text: order-service thread pool (200) was entirely consumed by slow requests to user-service, preventing it from handling any requests.

subject: payment-service payment failure rate surged
description: payment-service success rate dropped from 99.9% to 12%. Two issues: 1) Depends on Redis for payment idempotency checks -- Redis unavailability caused idempotency check failures; 2) After payment completion, needs to callback order-service to update order status -- order-service timeout caused callback failures. payment-service's own processing logic is normal; issues entirely caused by upstream/downstream dependency failures.

comments.author: Payment Team
comments.text: Recommend adding local idempotency cache as degradation fallback when Redis is unavailable.
